```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Natural gas demand spike from unseasonable cold snap detection",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",

      "source": "Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly for US population centers through weather_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true

    "behavior": "Daily minimum temperatures drop >15% below 30-day rolling average in winter months, signaling heating demand surge and natural gas consumption pressure.",

      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",

      "entry": "When US population-weighted temperature anomaly drops below -2 standard deviations from seasonal norm for 2+ consecutive days",
      "exit": "When temperature anomaly recovers above -1 standard deviation or after 7 trading days",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Winter weather volatility is seasonal and repeats reliably; unseasonable cold snaps occur multiple times per winter season."
  },
```
